query: Angular template compilation and binding Ivy create update AOT

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No single-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this exact Angular prompt; the available Angular basics coverage is broader than the question-specific comparison needed here.

competitors.label: Angular AOT Compiler Guide
competitors.selectionReason: The AOT compiler guide is the canonical source for Angular template compilation, metadata extraction, and generated code behavior.
theirStrengths: It explains AOT compilation, generated factories, and the benefits of catching template issues earlier.
theirGaps: It is compiler-focused and less interview-shaped around the create/update mental model used to debug bindings.

realWorldUseCases: many template bugs are really binding or change-detection debug problems, not “Angular magic.”
followUpConfusion: AOT answers when Angular compiles the template. Ivy create/update instructions answer what runtime code executes after compilation.

competitors.winReason: The AOT guide remains the canonical compiler reference, but our page now wins on the narrower interview follow-up: how compiled create/update work explains stale UI and why AOT timing is not the same thing as runtime update behavior.

realWorldUseCases: The docs connect compilation to runtime performance and earlier error detection, so the practical framing stays close.
actionableExamples: The AOT guide provides stronger compiler-specific examples and terminology than our compact trivia page.
followUpConfusion: It explains compilation phases well, but it is less compact than our answer on how update instructions re-read component state.

actionableExamples: Keep the stale-UI worked example next to the create/update pseudocode so the page stays more debug-oriented even if the compiler guide remains deeper on internals.

competitors.label: Telerik Angular Templates Overview
competitors.selectionReason: This tutorial is a strong broad competitor because it explains templates, bindings, and directives as the bridge between component state and the rendered view.
theirStrengths: It gives a broad explanation of templates, data binding, and how template syntax shapes the rendered UI.
theirGaps: It is more introductory and less explicit about the Ivy create/update compilation model.

realWorldUseCases: many template bugs are really binding or change-detection debug problems, not “Angular magic.”
actionableExamples: Simplified update work Angular reruns on each check
The usual bug is that the state change or update trigger did not reach this update phase.

competitors.winReason: Telerik is a solid templates overview, but our page now wins on the worked binding-debug example that maps `ctx` reads directly to DOM patches instead of stopping at template syntax basics.

realWorldUseCases: Telerik explains templates as the bridge between data and view, so the practical comparison is close.
actionableExamples: Its examples cover template syntax and binding basics clearly enough that the example comparison remains close.
followUpConfusion: Because it stays broad across templates, directives, and bindings, it gives more surrounding context to readers who are still shaky on template syntax itself.
Keep the line that separates compilation time from runtime update passes so the page does not slide back into a general templates overview.

competitors.label: Swiftorial Angular Templates
competitors.selectionReason: This is a direct tutorial competitor focused on Angular templates, bindings, and directives in one page.
theirStrengths: It directly explains templates, interpolation, property binding, event binding, and directives with small examples.
theirGaps: It stays at the template-syntax level and does not explain the create/update runtime model or why that matters in debugging.

realWorldUseCases: many template bugs are really binding or change-detection debug problems, not “Angular magic.”
actionableExamples: If the UI stays stale, Angular usually isn't "re-reading the template string" incorrectly.

competitors.winReason: Swiftorial explains template syntax well, but our page now wins on the under-the-hood debugging model and the concrete stale-UI example that ties create/update behavior back to real troubleshooting.

realWorldUseCases: Swiftorial explains what templates and bindings are, but our page is stronger on why the compile-plus-update model matters when debugging real apps.
actionableExamples: Its binding examples are concrete enough that the example criterion stays close.
followUpConfusion: It covers template syntax clearly, but it does not go as far into the ctx/update mental model behind Angular bindings.
If this still feels close, add one more short note on how scope errors and missing imports fail before the update phase logic even matters.
